properties.title: Abbir Maius
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 F4 Abbir Maius

properties.title: Abdera
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 B5 Abdera

properties.title: Abella
properties.description: Abella was an ancient settlement of Campania of Samnite origin.

properties.title: Abellinum
properties.description: Abellinum was a settlement of the Hirpini in Campania.

properties.title: Abila
properties.description: Abila was a Ptolemaic town located in northern Palestine. Antiochus III conquered it and Pompeius Magnus later freed it in 64 B.C. Becoming autonomous thereafter, Abila became part of the Decapolis.

properties.title: Abitinae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E3 Abitina(e)

properties.title: Abthugni
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E4 Abthugni

properties.title: Acerrae
properties.description: Acerrae was an ancient Oscan city of Campania, perhaps originally named Akeru. The Romans granted it civitas sine suffragio in 332 BC.

properties.title: Acmonia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 C4 Akmonia

properties.title: Acraephia
properties.description: An ancient town of Boeotia located on the eastern edge of the former Lake Copais.

properties.title: Acruvium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 F7 *Agruvium

properties.title: Ad Sava
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 C4 Ad Sava Municipium

properties.title: Adada
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 E2 Adada

properties.title: Adana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 G3 Adana/Antiochia ad Sarum

properties.title: Adraha
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 D4 Adra(h)a

properties.title: Adramyttium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 D2 Adramyttium

properties.title: Adranum
properties.description: An indigenous center of the eighth to fourth centuries B.C. that became Hellenized, Monte Adranone in Sicily is probably to be identified with the ancient name Adranon.

properties.title: Aeclanum
properties.description: An ancient settlement in Samnium that came to be associated with the Hirpini.

properties.title: Aegae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 Aegae

properties.title: Aegira
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 C1 Aigeira

properties.title: Aegium
properties.description: A city-state of ancient Achaea that numbered as one of the 12 Achaean cities. The city was located 30 stades from Rhypae, and 40 stades from Helice.

properties.title: Aelana
properties.description: The city of Aqaba on the Red Sea.

properties.title: Aelia Hadriana
properties.description: Iconium/Claudiconium (modern Konya) was an important city in Lycaonia that later became part of the Roman province of Galatia.

properties.title: Aeminium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 C4 Aeminium

properties.title: Aenona
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C5 Aenona

properties.title: Aenus
properties.description: An ancient city located at modern Enez in Turkey, Ainos was probably an Aeolian colony sent from Mytilene or Cyme in the 7th century BCE.

properties.title: Aequi
properties.description: Aequiculi was a municipium in the territory of the Aequi.

properties.title: Aequum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 D6 Aequum

properties.title: Aesernia
properties.description: An ancient settlement (modern Isernia in Italy)

properties.title: Aesis
properties.description: An Umbrian town that became a colonia civium Romanorum under the name of Aesis in 247 B.C.

properties.title: Aezanis
properties.description: Aizanoi was an ancient Roman city located in western Anatolia.

properties.title: Agbia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D4 Agbia

properties.title: Aginnum
properties.description: Aginnum (modern Agen) was the capital of the Nitiobriges in Gaul.

properties.title: Agrigentum
properties.description: Akragas/Agrigentum was an ancient city of Sicily and is now known as Agrigento. The Archaeological Area of Agrigento was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1997.

properties.title: Agrippias
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 E2 Anthedon/Agrippias

properties.title: Aguntum
properties.description: Aguntum was an ancient settlement of Noricum that received municipal status under Claudius.

properties.title: Alabanda
properties.description: Alabanda/Antiocheia Chrysaoron was an ancient city of Caria.

properties.title: Alba Fucentia
properties.description: A town of the Aequi along the Marsic frontier where the Romans established a Latin colony in 304/3 B.C. Alba Fucens was a significant center of the middle republican period and became a place where Rome kept state prisoners.

properties.title: Albingaunum
properties.description: The fourth century B.C. capital of the Ingauni Ligures sided with Carthage during the Punic War but was reduced by Lucius Aemilius Paullus Macedonicus in 181 B.C. A treaty was struck with Rome; the city received Roman citizenship in 45 B.C.

properties.title: Albintimilium
properties.description: Alb(i)um Intimilium/Albintimilium (modern Ventimiglia) was the main settlement of the Ligurian tribe of the Intemelii.

properties.title: Aleria
properties.description: A city of Corsica with significant pre-historic, Greek, Etruscan, and Roman phases.

properties.title: Aletrium
properties.description: A Hernican hill town that formed part of a sixth century B.C. defensive league against Volscian and Samnite aggression and allied itself to Tarquinius Superbus.

properties.title: Alexandria Troas
properties.description: An ancient city of the Troad, founded as Antigoneia by Antigonos Monophthalmos. Lysimachos renamed the city Alexandria after 301 BCE. The city became part of the kingdom of Pergamon and, later, the Roman empire.

properties.title: Alinda
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F2 Alinda/Alexandria ad Latmum

properties.title: Allifae
properties.description: An ancient town of either Oscan or Samnite origin situated in the Volturno river valley.

properties.title: Alsium
properties.description: Alsium served as a port for Caere, together with Fregenae.

properties.title: Althiburos
properties.description: Althiburos was a city of Africa Proconsularis that became a municipium under Hadrian and a colonia during the third century A.D.

properties.title: Altinum
properties.description: Altinum was the capital of the Veneti of northern Italy. The site was destroyed by Attila in AD 452.

properties.title: Amastris
properties.description: An ancient settlement on the Black Sea coast in Asia Minor, modern Amasra in Turkey.

properties.title: Amathus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Amathous

properties.title: Ambracia
properties.description: Ambracia (modern Arta) was an ancient city of central Greece, founded as a colony of Corinth in the seventh century BC. It was located near the Ambracian Gulf along the course of the river Arachthos.

properties.title: Ameria
properties.description: Ameria, claimed by Cato the Elder as the oldest town in Umbria, was an important center that may have gained Roman municipal status as early as 338 B.C., following the Latin War.

properties.title: Amiternum
properties.description: Amiternum was an ancient settlement of the Sabines that Rome captured in 293 BC. The settlement was situated at the intersection of four Roman roads: the Via Caecilia, the Via Claudia Nova, and the two branches of the Via Salaria.

properties.title: Ammaedara
properties.description: An extensive ancient settlement and road station at modern Haidra in Tunisia with evidence for occupation from the Roman, Vandal, and Byzantine eras. The site seems to have been abandoned by the 10th century CE.

properties.title: Amorium
properties.description: A Hellenistic and Roman settlement that eventually prospered under the Byzantine Empire, Amorion began minting its own coins in the second century BCE. Its decline comes following a sacking in the ninth century at the hands of Caliph Al-Mu'tasim.

properties.title: Amphissa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Amphissa

properties.title: Anchialus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 E6 Aulaiouteichos/Agathopolis

properties.title: Andautonia
properties.description: A Roman settlement and municipium near the Sava river, on the location of modern village of Šćitarjevo, Croatia, south from Zagreb, near Velika Gorica. Cited: BAtlas 20 D4 Andautonia

properties.title: Andematunum
properties.description: A Gallo-Roman city and capital of the Lingones

properties.title: Anemurium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 B4 Anemurium

properties.title: Antaeopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 F3 Antaiopolis/Tkoou

properties.title: Anthedon
properties.description: A port city of northern Boeotia located in the foothills of Mount Messapion on the Gulf of Euboea.

properties.title: Anticyra
properties.description: Antikyra was a port city located east of Kirrha. Pausanias identifies it with the Homeric Kyparissos.

properties.title: Antinoopolis
properties.description: An ancient city in Egypt established by Hadrian in A.D. 130 in honor of Antinous. The city stood on the east bank of the Nile, opposite Hermopolis.

properties.title: Antinum
properties.description: A town of the Marsi in the valley of the Liris river.

properties.title: Antiochia ad Chrysorhoam
properties.description: Gerasa (modern Jerash in Jordan) became a substantial center under Antiochos IV Epiphanes, at which time it became known as Antioch on the Chrysorhoas.

properties.title: Antiochia ad Maeandrum
properties.description: Antiochia ad Maeandrum was an ancient city in Caria located near Basaran, Aydin, Turkey.

properties.title: Antipatris
properties.description: Pegai/Antipatris/Arethousa? was a city built by Herod the Great and named for his father-in-law, Antipater.

properties.title: Anxanum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 G4 Anxanum

properties.title: Apamea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 F2 Apamea

properties.title: Aphrodisias
properties.description: Aphrodisias was a Greek city located in Caria. It is notable especially for the state of its archaeological preservation. The site was inscribed on UNESCO's World Heritage List in July 2017.

properties.title: Aphroditopolis
properties.description: Pathyris/Aphroditopolis is known especially for its necropolis where material ranges in date from the Predynastic Period to the Middle Kingdom.

properties.title: Apollonia
properties.description: Apollonia/Sozousa was a coastal town located between Jaffa and Caesarea. It should not be confused with the similarly named ancient site located in Cyrenaica.

properties.title: Apollonia
properties.description: Apollonia was an ancient city in the Cyrenaica and a port of Cyrene. The city was one of the five cities of the Libyan Pentapolis in Cyrenaica.

properties.title: Apollonia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D1 Apollonia/Mordiaion/Sozopolis

properties.title: Apollonia ad Rhyndacum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 52 D4 Apollonia ad Rhyndacum

properties.title: Apollonia Pontica
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 E6 Apollonia Pontica/Sozopolis

properties.title: Apollonopolis Magna
properties.description: The city of Apollonopolis Magna was located on the west bank of the Nile, south of Thebes. As the seat of Horus, Greeks regarded it as a site connected with Apollo. It served as the capital of the second nome of Upper Egypt.

properties.title: Apollonopolis Parva
properties.description: The ancient settlement of Apollonopolis Parva/Diocletianopolis, modern Qus.

properties.title: Aquae Balissae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 E4 Aquae Balissae/Municipium Iasorum

properties.title: Aquae Calidae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 D4 Aquae Calidae

properties.title: Aquae Flaviae
properties.description: Aquae Flaviae (modern Chaves, Portugal).

properties.title: Aquae Sextiae
properties.description: Col. Aquae Sextiae (modern Aix-en-Provence) was founded by Caius Sextius Calvinus who placed the colony on the territory of the indigenous confederation of the Salluvii in 122 B.C.

properties.title: Arausio
properties.description: Founded as Colonia Julia Firma Secundanorum Arausio in 35 B.C.

properties.title: Ardea
properties.description: Ardea was an ancient settlement of the Rutuli in Latium.

properties.title: Argentomagus
properties.description: *Argentomagus was a pre-Roman and later Roman settlement in the territory of the Bituriges tribe.

properties.title: Argentoratum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 H4 Argentovaria?

properties.title: Ariassus
properties.description: Ariassos is located in Pisidia and is mentioned by several ancient sources, including Ptolemy.

properties.title: Aricia
properties.description: Aricia is an ancient Latin settlement and a member of the Latin League.

properties.title: Ariminum
properties.description: Ariminum (Rimini) is a Roman colony founded on the Adriatic coast in 268 BC.

properties.title: Arna
properties.description: Arna was an ancient city of Umbria that was controlled by Etruscans in the fourth century BCE.

properties.title: Arpinum
properties.description: An ancient center that originated as early as the seventh century B.C. that became a civitas sine suffragio in 305 B.C. Arpinum was the birthplace of Caius Marius and Marcus Tullius Cicero.

properties.title: Arsennaria
properties.description: A settlement in the Roman province of Mauretania Caesariensis in North Africa.

properties.title: Arva
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E4 Arva

properties.title: Arycanda
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D4 Arykanda

properties.title: Asido
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E5 Asido

properties.title: Asisium
properties.description: A Roman settlement built over an Umbrian one on a western foothill of the Apennines, Asisium received municipal status and was assigned to the Tribus Sergia. Totila destroyed most of the city in A.D. 545.

properties.title: Aspendus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 F4 Aspendos/Primoupolis

properties.title: Assuras
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 D1 Assuras

properties.title: Assus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Assos/Apollonia

properties.title: Atella
properties.description: A city of Campania located between Capua and Neapolis.

properties.title: Aternum
properties.description: Aternum (modern Pescara) was a settlement at the mouth of the Aternus river.

properties.title: Athribis
properties.description: An ancient city of Lower Egypt located at present-day Tell Atrib to the northeast of Benha on the hill of Kom Sidi Yusuf.

properties.title: Atina
properties.description: Atina was a Volscian settlement located at the source of the river Melpis. Atina was located on the via Latina between Casinum and Sora.

properties.title: Atrax
properties.description: An ancient Greek polis in Pelasgiotis in Thessaly. The site is located in modern Greece, immediately south of the Peneios river and 2.5 km west of a small village named Κάστρο (Kastro), which is within the municipality of Larissa.

properties.title: Atria
properties.description: An ancient city of Picenum that may have originated as colony established by Etruscans from Adria in the Veneto region. An alternate narrative suggests that the city's origins may be as a late sixth century B.C. colony founded by Aegina.

properties.title: Attalea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 F3 Attaleia

properties.title: Aufidena
properties.description: A probable Samnite center, the Romans captured Aufidena in 298 BC. It only became a municipium after 49 B.C.

properties.title: Augusta
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Diocletianopolis

properties.title: Augusta Praetoria
properties.description: Augusta Praetoria (modern Aosta) was a settlement of the Salassi conquered by Rome in 25 B.C. From 24 B.C. a colony of 3,000 Roman military veterans occupied the city of Augusta Praetoria Salassorum.

properties.title: Augusta Raurica
properties.description: Col. Augusta Raurica (modern Augst) was a Roman colony on the south bank of the Rhine established by Iulius Caesar and L. Munatius Plancus in 44 B.C.

properties.title: Augusta Taurinorum
properties.description: Augusta Taurinorum (modern Turin), the settlement of indigenous Celto-Ligurian peoples, was re-settled as a Roman veteran colony in 28 BC.

properties.title: Augusta Traiana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 C6 Beroe/Augusta Traiana

properties.title: Augusta Tricastinorum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D5 Noiomagos/Col. Augusta Tricastinorum

properties.title: Augustonemetum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 Augustonemetum

properties.title: Augustoritum
properties.description: Augustoritum (modern Limoges, France) in Gaul was founded as a Roman city ca. 10 B.C.

properties.title: Aurgi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 B4 Aurgi/Orongis

properties.title: Auximum
properties.description: Auximum (modern Osimo) is an ancient settlement of Picenum. The original foundation was likely carried out by Greeks from Ancona. The Romans made it a base for military operations from 174 B.C.

properties.title: Auzia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 G4 Auzia

properties.title: Aveia
properties.description: Aveia was a settlement of the Vestini in the valley of the Aterno river.

properties.title: Aventicum
properties.description: An ancient settlement of the Helvetii, located between Aare and Lake Geneva. Modern Avenches in Switzerland.

properties.title: Avitta Bibba
properties.description: Avitta Bibba was a settlement located in the Roman province of Africa Proconsularis.

properties.title: Axati
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E4 Axati

properties.title: Azotos
properties.description: An ancient settlement of the Philistines.

properties.title: Baelo
properties.description: B(a)elo is located on the northern shore of the Strait of Gibraltar. The town was founded by the late second century BC and achieved municipal status under Claudius.

properties.title: Baeterrae
properties.description: Colonia Julia Baeterrae Septimanorum is located where the Via Herculia crosses the Orb river. It is now modern Béziers, France.  The site has been inhabited at least since 750-650 B.C.

properties.title: Baetulo
properties.description: Baetulo (now Badalona in Spain) was situated near the mouth of the Baetulo river some 10 km northeast of Barcelona. The likely native oppidum became a Roman settlement.

properties.title: Balbura
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C4 Balboura

properties.title: Baloie
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 E5 Baloie

properties.title: Balsa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 C4 Balsa

properties.title: Banasa
properties.description: Colonia Iulia Valentia Banasa was one of the three colonies founded by emperor Augustus between 33 and 25 B.C. in Mauretania Tingitana.

properties.title: Bararus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 G2 Bararus

properties.title: Barce
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 B1 Barke

properties.title: Bargylia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F3 Bargylia

properties.title: Bassiana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 D2 Bassiana

properties.title: Bassiana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 B5 Bassiana

properties.title: Batnae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 G3 Batnae/Markopolis

properties.title: Belalis Maior
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D3 Belalis Maior

properties.title: Berenice
properties.description: Berenice was a coastal city of western Cyrenaica, located at modern Benghazi in Libya. Founded in the third century BCE, it replaced the earlier Greek colony of Eusperides, which had been centered a short distance to the northeast.

properties.title: Bergomum
properties.description: Originally a center of the archaic Golasecca culture, Bergomum became a municipium of the tribus Voturia. In 452 Attila captured and razed the city.

properties.title: Beroea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 50 B3 Ber(r)oia

properties.title: Beroea
properties.description: The ancient city of Aleppo, located in modern Syria, where extensive elements of Hittite, Hellenistic, Roman, Byzantine, Ayyubid, and Umayyad architecture are incorporated into the urban fabric of the modern city.

properties.title: Bida
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 H3 Bida

properties.title: Bilbilis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D4 Bilbilis (Augusta)

properties.title: Bisica Lucana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E4 Bisica Lucana

properties.title: Bistua Nova
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 E5 Bistua Nova

properties.title: Bizye
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 52 B1 Bizye

properties.title: Blaundus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 B5 Blaundos

properties.title: Blera
properties.description: Blera was a significant Etruscan settlement in the vicinity of Tarquinii. It lay along the line of the Roman Via Clodia. It received Roman municipal status and was enrolled in the tribus Arnensis.

properties.title: Bononia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 E5 Bononia

properties.title: Bononia
properties.description: A city of the Po Plain with important cultural phases related to Etruscan, Celtic, and Roman culture.

properties.title: Bovianum
properties.description: Bovianum was a Samnite center and the capital of the Pentri tribe. It received Roman municipium status ca. 87 B.C. By the late first century A.D. it was occupied by veterans of Legio XI and known as the "colonia Bovianum Undecimanoruin".

properties.title: Brigetio
properties.description: Brigetio was a legionary camp, cannabae, and municipium along the Pannonian limes.

properties.title: Brundisium
properties.description: A Greek and Roman port, terminus of the Via Appia.

properties.title: Bubon
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 B4 Boubon

properties.title: Burnum
properties.description: From ca. 6 B.C. Burnum was the site of a Roman legionary camp (Legio XI; Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis - Legio IIII Flavia Felix and Legio VIII Augusta), as well as an auxiliary camp.

properties.title: Byblos
properties.description: Occupied since the Neolithic period, historical Byblos was originally a major Punic center called Gebal.

properties.title: Byllis
properties.description: A Hellenistic and Roman site located on the right bank of the Aous river with a defensive circuit of ca. 2 km.

properties.title: Cabyle
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 D6 Kabyle/Diospolis

properties.title: Caesarea ad Libanum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B4 Arca/Herakleia en Phoinike/Caesarea ad Libanum

properties.title: Caesarea Philippi
properties.description: An ancient settlement at the southwest foot of Mount Hermon, located in the Israeli-occupied Golan Heights, just across the Lebanese border.

properties.title: Caesarodunum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F1 Caesarodunum

properties.title: Caiatia
properties.description: A Samnite town that eventually became a Roman municipium of the tribus Falerna ca. 89 B.C.

properties.title: Calagurris
properties.description: Calagurris (Nassica) Iulia (modern Calahorra, Spain) was conquered by Rome in 187 BC and later supported Quintus Sertorius in his war against Cn. Pompeius Magnus.

properties.title: Calama
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 G4 Calama

properties.title: Calatia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F3 Calatia

properties.title: Cales
properties.description: Cales was an ancient city of Campania of Ausonian origins. The Romans captured the town in 335 B.C. and established a colony there.

properties.title: Callatis
properties.description: A Greek colony and port on the west coast of the Black Sea, modern Mangalia in Romania.

properties.title: Callet
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E4 Callet

properties.title: Calleva
properties.description: Calleva Atrebatum (known as Silchester Roman Town) was an Iron Age oppidum and Roman town in Britannia. It was the civitas capital of the Atrebates tribe.

properties.title: Calpe
properties.description: A Phoenician and Roman town in Spain located on the Bay of Gibraltar. The site played a strategic role in the Punic Wars, and was given the rights of a Latin colony in 171 BC.

properties.title: Calydon
properties.description: An ancient Greek city of Aetolia that derives its name from its founder Calydon, son of Aetolus.

properties.title: Camarina
properties.description: Camarina was founded by Syracuse in 599 B.C., but subsequently destroyed in 552 B.C. The city was re-founded by Gela in 461 B.C. Carthage razed the site in 405 B.C., but Timoleon restored it in 339 B.C.

properties.title: Cambodunum
properties.description: Cambodunum was a Celtic settlement of Raetia. A Roman presence began under Tiberius (first century AD) when a Roman garrison was stationed at the site.

properties.title: Camerinum
properties.description: Camerinum was a town in Umbria on the frontiers of Picenum that was allied to Rome from 309 B.C.

properties.title: Camulodunum
properties.description: Col. Camulodunum (Colchester) was the pre-Roman capital of the Trinovantes; it received a Roman city after the annexation of Britannia.

properties.title: Canama
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E4 Canama

properties.title: Canatha
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 E4 Canatha/*Gabinia/*Septimia

properties.title: Canusium
properties.description: A center of the ancient Daunians, Canusium became allied to Rome in 318 B.C. It received municipal status in 88 B.C.

properties.title: Capena
properties.description: An ancient urban center in Faliscan territory adjacent to Etruria, Capena was sacked by the Romans in 390 BC.

properties.title: Capera
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 E4 Capera

properties.title: Capsa
properties.description: Capsa (Iustiniana) was an ancient city of Byzacium in north Africa.

properties.title: Carissa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E5 Caris(s)a

properties.title: Carmo
properties.description: Carmo was a town in Hispania Ulterior.

properties.title: Carpasium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 E1 Karpasia

properties.title: Carpis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 G3 Carpi(s)

properties.title: Carrhae
properties.description: Carrhae is a major settlement of Upper Mesopotamia from the Bronze Age onward. The earliest mentions of the site are found in the Ebla tablets (ca. 2300 B.C.).

properties.title: Carseoli
properties.description: An ancient settlement along the Via Valeria, Carsioli was established as a Latin colony in the territory of the Aequi ca. 298 BC.

properties.title: Carsulae
properties.description: An ancient settlement originating the late fourth or third centuries BC and lying along the route of the Roman Via Flaminia.

properties.title: Cartenna
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 B3 Cartenna

properties.title: Cartima
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 A5 Cartima

properties.title: Casilinum
properties.description: A settlement located in the border region between the ager Falernus and the ager Campanus, north of Capua.

properties.title: Castabala
properties.description: A city of Cilicia with Hittite origins, Hierapolis/Kastabala later became part of the Cilician satrapy of the Achaemenid Empire and, later, part of the Roman empire.

properties.title: Castrum Novum
properties.description: Castrum Novum was a colonia maritima established on the coast of Etruria.

properties.title: Castrum Novum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 F3 Castrum Novum

properties.title: Castrum Truentinum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 F3 Castrum Truentinum

properties.title: Castulo
properties.description: An Ibero-Roman settlement of Baetica that was inhabited beginning in the Neolithic period. It is noted for the silver mines of the Sierra Morena.

properties.title: Catania
properties.description: The colonial city of Katane (modern Catania) was founded by Chalcidian Greeks sometime after the foundation of Leontini in 730 B.C.

properties.title: Caudium
properties.description: A city of Samnium located between Capua and Beneventum. In 321 B.C. the Samnites under C. Pontius encamped there before defeating the Romans at the battle of the Caudine Forks.

properties.title: Caunus
properties.description: An ancient city of Caria in southwest Asia Minor.

properties.title: Celeia
properties.description: Celeia was an important settlement in Noricum.

properties.title: Celenderis
properties.description: An ancient city on the southern coast of Asia Minor at modern Aydıncık in Turkey's Mersin province. It was a major pottery producer and exporter during the Achaemenid period.

properties.title: Celsa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 E4 Celsa/Col. Victrix Iulia Lepida

properties.title: Cenabum
properties.description: Cenabum was an oppidum of the Carnutes tribe.

properties.title: Centuripae
properties.description: A city of the indigenous Sikels, Centuripae allied itself with Athens during the expedition against Syracuse.

properties.title: Cereatae Marianae
properties.description: Cereatae Marianae, purported to have been the birthplace of Gaius Marius, is thought to have been located where the 13th century abbey church of Casamari now stands (in the modern province of Frosinone, Lazio, Italy).

properties.title: Chaeronea
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Boeotia, Chaeronea was a subject of Orchomenus and a member of the Boeotian League. From the late fifth century BC, Chaeronea belonged to one of the 11 Boeotian districts.

properties.title: Chalcedon
properties.description: Chalcedon was an ancient maritime city of Bithynia.

properties.title: Chersonasos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 D2 Chersonasos

properties.title: Chios
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C5 Chios

properties.title: Choba
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 C3 Choba

properties.title: Chullu
properties.description: An ancient city of Numidia located on the Sinus Olcachites.

properties.title: Chythrus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Chyt(h)roi

properties.title: Cibalae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 F4 Cibalae

properties.title: Cibyra
properties.description: An ancient Greek city, largely rebuilt in the early Roman empire after a major earthquake. Kibyra is located near the modern town of Göhlisar (formerly Horzum) in Turkey's Burdur province.

properties.title: Cierium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C2 Kierion

properties.title: Cillium
properties.description: Cillium (modern Kasserine, Tunisia) occupies a strategic location in the gap between the Semmama and Chambi mountains, along the road from Gafsa.

properties.title: Cingulum
properties.description: Cingulum was a town in Picenum.

properties.title: Circeii
properties.description: A port on the Tyrrhenian coast. In myth, the site was considered to be one of the stopping points of the hero Odysseus.

properties.title: Cirta
properties.description: The capital city of the ancient Berber Kingdom of Numidia.

properties.title: Cisamus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 A2 Kisamos

properties.title: Citium
properties.description: Kition was an ancient city founded on the southern coast of Cyprus in the thirteenth century B.C.

properties.title: Civitas Nattabutum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 G4 Civitas Nattabutum/Castellum Ro(...)

properties.title: Claudia Aprensis
properties.alt: Aproi, Aprus, Col. Claudia Aprensis, Nova Theodosiopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 52 A3 Aproi/Col. Claudia Aprensis/Nova Theodosiopolis

properties.title: Claudia Archelais
properties.description: Originally known as Garsaura, this place was a key settlement of the strategia of Garsaouritis. King Archelaus refounded it and named it after himself. Under the Roman emperor Claudius the site received colonial status.

properties.title: Claudia Caes. Ptol.
properties.description: The Phoenician city of Akko was re-founded by Ptolemy II Philadelphos from ca. 285-246 B.C.

properties.title: Claudiopolis
properties.description: An ancient settlement in Bithynia, modern Bolu in Turkey.

properties.title: Claudioseleucia
properties.description: Seleucia Sidera was an ancient city in the northern part of Pisidia, Anatolia, founded by Seleucus I Nicator or Antiochus I Soter in order to protect the military road across northern Pisidia.

properties.title: Clazomenae
properties.description: A town in Lydia that, according to Pausanias, moved from an initial, mainland site thought by scholars to have been southwest of modern Urla Iskelesi to an island in the Bay of Smyrna (modern Klazümen).

properties.title: Cleitor
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 C2 Kleitor

properties.title: Cluviae
properties.description: A city of the Carricini Samnites.

properties.title: Clysma
properties.description: Clysma was an ancient port city at the northern end of the Gulf of Suez (modern al‑Qulzum in the city of Suez).

properties.title: Cnidus
properties.description: An ancient settlement located at Tekir on the western tip of the modern Datça peninsula in Turkey (ancient Knidia), which extends from the southwest corner of Asia Minor.

properties.title: Colophon
properties.description: Colophon was an Ionian city in Lydia, located ca. 13 km north of the harbour of Notion. Its ruins are located near modern Değirmendere.

properties.title: Colossae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 B2 Colossae

properties.title: Comama
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D3 Comama/Col. Iulia Augusta Prima Fida

properties.title: Comana
properties.description: Comana/Hierapolis/Chryse was an ancient city in Cappadocia.

properties.title: Comum
properties.description: Comum (modern Como) was a key city of regio XI of ancient Italy. The Celtic site received a Roman colony (Novum Comum) under Iulius Caesar in the first century BC.

properties.title: Conimbriga
properties.description: Conimbriga was a Celtic and Roman settlement in Portugal that was inhabited between the ninth century BC and the seventh century AD.

properties.title: Contributa Iulia Ugultunia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D3 Contributa Iulia Ugultunia

properties.title: Coptos
properties.description: An ancient settlement on the Nile river (modern Quft/Qift in Egypt) that flourished from pre-dynastic times through late antiquity.

properties.title: Cora
properties.description: A Latin center in the Monti Lepini that was already a Roman municipium by 211 B.C.

properties.title: Coracesium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 G4 Korakesion

properties.title: Corcyra
properties.description: The eponymous settlement of the island of Corcyra (modern Korfu).

properties.title: Corfinium
properties.description: Corfinium was the principal city of the Paeligni in the valley of the Aternus river. The site becomes the capital of the breakaway Italic groups that oppose Rome during the Social War.

properties.title: Corinium Dobunnorum
properties.description: Korinion/Cironium (also referred to as Corinium Dobunnorum) was the second largest town of Roman Britain.

properties.title: Coronea
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Boeotia, located about 4 km north of the modern Greek city that now bears its name.

properties.title: Cortona
properties.description: An Umbrian city that, under Etruscan influence, expanded and came to be called 'Curtun'. Later it became the Roman colony of Cortino.

properties.title: Corycus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D5 Korykos

properties.title: Cosa
properties.description: A Latin colony in south Etruria founded ca. 273 B.C.

properties.title: Cremna
properties.description: A significant Hellenistic settlement of Pisidia that received an Augustan colony, taking the name Colonia Iulia Augusta Felix Cremnensium.

properties.title: Cremona
properties.description: Cremona is a city of Cisalpine Gaul located on the Po river. The Romans established a colony of 6,000 settlers there after 225 B.C. Cremona was the first Latin colony placed north of the Po river.

properties.title: Croton
properties.description: An Achaean colony in Magna Graecia established in 710 BC by the oikist Myscellus from the city of Rhypes in the Peloponnese.

properties.title: Cubulteria
properties.description: Kupelternum/Cubulteria was an ancient Samnite settlement.

properties.title: Cuicul
properties.description: A Berbero-Roman town of North Africa (modern Djémila in Algeria). Sometimes labeled on maps and in travel information as "Curculum".

properties.title: Cumae
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Campania, Cumae was the first Greek colony established on the Italian mainland in the eighth century BC.

properties.title: Cupra Maritima
properties.description: Cupra Maritima was a coastal city of Picenum (regio V) assigned to the tribus Velina.

properties.title: Cures
properties.description: Cures (also Cures Sabini) was a Sabine city located in the Tiber river valley along the Via Salaria. The Romans conquered Cures in 290 B.C. under M'. Curius Dentatus and granted it status as a municipium in the tribus Sergia.

properties.title: Curium
properties.description: An ancient settlement in the southern part of the island of Cyprus, located southwest of Episkopi in the Lemesos district.

properties.title: Cyaneae
properties.description: An ancient city of Lycia and the chief settlement in the region between Antiphellos and Myra.

properties.title: Cyme
properties.description: An ancient city of Aeolis.

properties.title: Cyrrus
properties.description: Seleucid kings (either Seleucus I Nicator or Antiochus I Soter), likely founded Cyrrhus in the first half of the third century B.C.

properties.title: Dardanorum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 C6 Dard(...)

properties.title: Darnis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Darnis

properties.title: Dea Vocontiorum
properties.description: An Augustan colony in Gallia Narbonensis located in the valley of the Drôme river; an important cultic center of the Vocontii.

properties.title: Delminium
properties.description: Delminium was an ancient city of Illyria and a capital of the Dalmatae.

properties.title: Dertona
properties.description: A city founded in the second century B.C. at the junction of the Via Postumia and Via Aemilia Scauri which merged to become the Via Julia Augusta.

properties.title: Dertosa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F5 Dertosa/Hibera Iulia Ilercavonia

properties.title: Deultum
properties.description: An ancient settlement, modern Debelt in Bulgaria.

properties.title: Diana Veteranorum
properties.description: Diana Veteranorum was a vicus in Numidia founded for veterans of Legio III Augusta. It became a municipium in A.D. 162.

properties.title: Dianium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 F3 Dianium/Hemeroskopeion

properties.title: Dierna
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 E5 Dierna

properties.title: Diluntum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 E6 Diluntum

properties.title: Diocaesarea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B4 Sepphoris/Diocaesarea

properties.title: Diocaesarea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 D3 Diocaesarea

properties.title: Dionysias
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 E4 Dionysias/*Soada

properties.title: Diospolis Parva
properties.description: The capital of the seventh Nome of Upper Egypt, located on the west bank of the Nile.

properties.title: Dium
properties.description: Dion was a settlement of Pieria located just south of Macedonia and held, traditionally, as the place of Orpheus' death and burial.

properties.title: Divona
properties.description: Divona (Cahors) was the capital of the civitas of the Cadurci throughout the Roman period. It takes its name from a sacred spring (likely the Fontaine des Chartreux) and a ford on the Lot river.

properties.title: Doclea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 B1 Doclea

properties.title: Domavium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 B5 Domavium

properties.title: Dora
properties.description: A coastal settlement of Phoenicia, now Tel Dor to the south of Haifa.

properties.title: Dorylaion
properties.description: Dorylaion was an important city in the north of Phrygia (modern Eskişehir in Turkey).

properties.title: Drobeta
properties.description: Drobeta is a Dacian settlement that was conquered by the Romans in A.D. 101-2. Trajan's engineer Apollodorus of Damascus built a bridge here that spanned the Danube in 103-5.

properties.title: Durnovaria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E4 Durno(no)varia

properties.title: Durovernum
properties.description: Durovernum Cantiacorum, modern Canterbury, was the site of a pre-Roman oppidum and, then, post A.D. 43 of a Roman fort.

properties.title: Dyme
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 B1 Dyme

properties.title: Ebora
properties.description: A Roman city with likely Celtic roots and a long, subsequent architectural history, the historic center of modern Évora in Portugal was added to the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1986.

properties.title: Ebusus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 G3 Ebusus

properties.title: Edessa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 50 B3 Edessa

properties.title: Egitania
properties.description: A Roman city whose name, Egitania (Civitas Igaeditanorum), was bestowed by the Swabians and Visigoths. This settlement achieved municipal status under the Flavian emperors.

properties.title: Elaea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 Elaea

properties.title: Elephantine
properties.description: A fortified settlement located just before the First Cataract of the Nile. During the Second Intermediate Period, Elephantine marked the southern border of Egypt.

properties.title: Eleusis
properties.description: Eleusis, located some 22 km west of Athens, was the site of the cult of Demeter and the sanctuary in which the Eleusinian Mysteries were celebrated from the Bronze Age to the Roman Imperial period.

properties.title: Elusa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 F3 Elousa

properties.title: Emesa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 C4 (H)Emesa

properties.title: Emona
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B3 Emona

properties.title: Emporiae
properties.description: A major economic center in the Iberian peninsula, Emporiae (modern Empúries) had indigenous origins but would be the site of a large Greek colonial city.

properties.title: Enna
properties.description: The origins of (H)Enna are unclear. Some sources claim it was founded by Syracuse in 664 B.C. but the archaeological record suggests it is likely a Hellenized Sikel settlement.

properties.title: Epidaurum
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Illyria on the Adriatic coast. Modern Cavtat in Croatia.

properties.title: Epiphaneia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 C3 Oeniandos/Epiphaneia

properties.title: Eporedia
properties.description: A Roman military outpost during the Roman invasion of the Dora Baltea ca. 100 B.C., Eporedia was assigned to Regio XI.

properties.title: Eryx
properties.description: An ancient, indigenous settlement of western Sicily, likely inhabited by the Elymians.

properties.title: Euesperides
properties.description: Euesperides was the westernmost Greek colony of Cyrenaica. The remains of the site are found on the outskirts of modern Benghazi near the north Libyan coast.

properties.title: Euonymon
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 59 B3 Euonymon

properties.title: Fabrateria Nova
properties.description: Fabrateria Nova received a Roman colony in 124 B.C. that was dispatched by Caius Gracchus. The relationship between Fabrateria Nova and the earlier Volscian site of Fabrateria Vetus remains unclear.

properties.title: Faesulae
properties.description: Faesulae (modern Fiesole) was an Etruscan foundation called Viesul, Viśl, or Vipsul. The city was conquered by Romans in 283 B.C. Sulla would settle Roman veterans there. Citizens of the town supported the conspiracy of Catiline.

properties.title: Fagifulae
properties.description: Fagifulae was a center of the Pentrian Samnites.

properties.title: Falerii Novi
properties.description: A fortified town located some 6 km from Falerii Veteres and established by the ancient Romans ca. 241 B.C.

properties.title: Falerio
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 E2 Falerio

properties.title: Fanum Fortunae
properties.description: Fanum Fortunae, the site of a shrine to Fortuna, is first mentioned in the ancient sources in 49 B.C. when Iulius Caesar held the town. An Augustan colony was established at the site and the Romans prevailed in the Battle of Fano in A.D. 271.

properties.title: Ferentinum
properties.description: A Hernican town along the Via Latina that the Romans captured in 364 B.C. Later in the Republican period (after 195 B.C.) the city received Roman municipal status.

properties.title: Ferentium
properties.description: Ferentium (modern Ferento) was a city of Etruria first mentioned in the Liber Coloniarum ca. 123 BC that became a Roman municipium after the Social War.

properties.title: Fidentia
properties.description: Fidentia (modern Fidenza) began as a Roman castrum and in 41 BC it became a municipium. The settlement is located along the Via Aemilia, between Parma and Placentia.

properties.title: Firmum Picenum
properties.description: Firmum Picenum was founded as a Latin colony in 264 B.C. with 6,000 colonists. It received municipal status after 48 B.C.

properties.title: Flanona
properties.description: Flanona (modern Plomin) was an ancient Roman settlement in Istria.

properties.title: Flavia Solva
properties.description: Flavia Solva was a Celtic-Roman settlement located on the banks of the Mur river in Noricum.

properties.title: Florentia
properties.description: Florentia (modern Florence / Firenze) was founded as a colony for military veterans ca. 80 B.C. by Lucius Cornelius Sulla.

properties.title: Formiae
properties.description: An ancient settlement of likely Greek origin along the Via Appia. Cicero was assassinated near Formiae (now Formia) in 43 B.C.

properties.title: Forum Hadriani
properties.description: Aurelium Cananefatium/Forum Hadriani (modern Voorburg) was the northernmost Roman city on the European continent. It was destroyed ca. AD 270.

properties.title: Forum Iulii
properties.description: Col. Forum Iulii (modern Fréjus) was a Roman military colony, most likely founded by Iulius Caesar, that served as a major naval base.

properties.title: Forum Iulii
properties.description: A settlement founded as a Roman municipium along the line of the Via Iulia Augusta by Julius Caesar ca. 50 BCE (cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Forum Iulii)

properties.title: Frusino
properties.description: Frusino (modern Frosinone) was a town in Latium along the Via Latina that was originally a Volscian settlement.

properties.title: Fulginiae
properties.description: Fulginiae was an ancient town of Umbria, Italy, on the later line of the Via Flaminia, ca. 20 km south of Nuceria.

properties.title: Fundi
properties.description: Fundi achieved civitas sine suffragio after the Latin War in 338 B.C.

properties.title: Gadara
properties.description: A Ptolemaic foundation in northern Jordan that eventually became part of the Decapolis.

properties.title: Gagae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D5 Gagai/Palaion Teichos

properties.title: Genetiva Iulia
properties.description: At the modern town of Osuna in Spain's Seville province, archaeological investigation has revealed an extensive Roman-era settlement. In 2022, an earlier "Phoenician" necropolis was discovered.

properties.title: Gigthis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 C1 Gigthis

properties.title: Glevum
properties.description: Site of a Roman legionary fortress (ca. 67 A.D.) and later colonia (founded ca. 97-98 A.D.) at modern day Gloucester.

properties.title: Gonnus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C1 Gonnoi

properties.title: Gorgippia
properties.description: Gorgippia/Sindikos Limen was a sixth century B.C. colony founded by Pontic Greeks on the site of Sinda.

properties.title: Gorsium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 F2 Gorsium/Herculia

properties.title: Graviscae
properties.description: The port of the Etruscan city of Tarquinia.

properties.title: Grumentum
properties.description: Grumentum was an ancient settlement of Lucania, likely inhabited since the sixth century B.C. The site played a role in the Second Punic War and later became a bishopric in the fourth century A.D.

properties.title: Gunugu
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 C3 Gunugu

properties.title: Gythium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 D4 Gyth(e)ion

properties.title: Hadrianopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 B1 Hadrianopolis/Kaukalou Kome?

properties.title: Hadrianopolis
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Lydia, located near modern Siledik in Turkey.

properties.title: Hadrianopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D5 Olympus/Hadrianopolis

properties.title: Hadrianoupolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 51 H1 Hadrianoupolis

properties.title: Hadrumetum
properties.description: Hadrumetum/Iustinianopolis was an important ancient port in North Africa. Founded by Phoenicians, the site pre-dated Carthage.

properties.title: Halicarnassus
properties.description: An ancient city in Caria, now known as Bodrum in Turkey.

properties.title: Halus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D2 Halos

properties.title: Hasta
properties.description: An ancient town near Jerez de la Frontera, which belonged to the Conventus Hispalensis.

properties.title: Heba
properties.description: A Roman colony that was established in the third or second century B.C., Heba lay near modern Magliano. Funerary evidence indicate previous Etruscan occupation at the site.

properties.title: Heliopolis
properties.description: Ancient Heliopolis was the capital of the 13th or Heliopolite Nome of Lower Egypt and served as a major religious center. The site is now located in Ayn Shams, a suburb of Cairo.

properties.title: Helvia Ricina
properties.description: An ancient settlement in Picenum at modern Villa Potenza in Marche, Comune di Macerata, Italy.

properties.title: Heraclea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Trachis/Herakleia

properties.title: Heraclea
properties.description: Heraclea was an ancient city of Magna Graecia.

properties.title: Heraclea
properties.description: Herakleia or Herakleia Lynkestis was an ancient city of Macedonia founded by Philip II of Macedon in the middle of the fourth century B.C.

properties.title: Heraclea ad Latmum
properties.description: A town of Caria located 25 km west of Miletos on the Ionian coast, Heraclea was located on the lower slopes of Mt. Latmos. Once a part of the Delian League, the city fell to Mausolos in the fourth century B.C.

properties.title: Heraclea Minoa
properties.description: An ancient Greek city of the south coast of Sicily located at the mouth of the Halykos river. Founded by Selinus in the sixth century BC, the site was abandoned in the first century AD.

properties.title: Heraclea Salbakes
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 A2 Herakleia Salbakes

properties.title: Heracleopolis Magna
properties.description: Herakleopolis (also called Herakleopolis Magna), was the captial of the Twentieth nome of Egypt.

properties.title: Herculaneum
properties.description: An ancient Roman town of Campania destroyed by the eruption of Mt. Vesuvius in A.D. 79. Named as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1997.

properties.title: Hermonthis
properties.description: An important town of the Egyptian Middle Kingdom that was enlarged during the Eighteenth Dynasty.

properties.title: Hermopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 E3 Hermopolis

properties.title: Hibis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 D3 Hibis

properties.title: Hierapolis
properties.description: Hierapolis was a major ancient city in Phrygia located at the site of hot springs in southwestern Anatolia. The site was designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1988.

properties.title: Himera
properties.description: A colony on the north shore of Sicily founded in 649/8 BC by the Myletidai.

properties.title: Hippo Diarrhytus
properties.description: Hippo Diarrhytus was an ancient port located on the coast of North Africa. The site is now occupied by the city of Bizerte in Tunisia.

properties.title: Hippus
properties.description: Hippos was an ancient city that flourished between the third century B.C. and the seventh century A.D. It was abandoned following an earthquake in 749.

properties.title: Hispellum
properties.description: Hispellum (modern Spello) is an ancient settlement of Umbria that became a Roman colony in the first century BC.

properties.title: Histonium
properties.description: Histonium, on the Adriatic coast, was a chief town of the Frentani.

properties.title: Histria
properties.description: Histria was a Greek colony founded near the mouths of the Danube ca. 630 BC.

properties.title: Horta
properties.description: The Etruscan settlement of *Hurta (the modern Orte) was close to the Vadimonis Lacus where the Romans fought, and won, two engagements against Etruscan armies.

properties.title: Iader
properties.description: The ancient Roman town of Iader (Iadera), given status of a Roman colony in 48 B.C.

properties.title: Iamo
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 inset Iamo/Iamna

properties.title: Iasus
properties.description: Iasos was an ancient Greek city in Caria.

properties.title: Icosium
properties.description: Icosium (modern Algiers) was a Punic and, later, Roman settlement in coastal north Africa. Legend holds that 20 of Herakles' companions founded the original town.

properties.title: Igilgili
properties.description: Originally a Carthaginian trading post, Igilgili eventually came to be part of Mauretania Caesariensis as a Roman colony.

properties.title: Iguvium
properties.description: An Umbrian hilltown, Iguvium is best known as the find-spot of the Iguvine Tables.

properties.title: Ilerda
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Ilerda

properties.title: Ilici
properties.description: The site of Ilici began as a pre-Roman settlement that eventually developed into a Roman municipium.

properties.title: Iliturgi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 B4 Iliturgi

properties.title: Ilium
properties.description: The "VIllage of the Ilians" located on the slopes of Karatepe in Asiatic Turkey.

properties.title: Ilugo
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 B3 Ilugo

properties.title: Iluro
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 F5 Iluro

properties.title: Interamna Lirenas
properties.description: A Latin colony founded ca. 312 B.C. along the Via Latina. The site was sacked by Samnites (ca. 294 B.C.) and subsequently by Hannibal (212 B.C.). It received Roman municipal status in 90 B.C.

properties.title: Interamna Nahars
properties.description: An Umbrian center founded in the seventh century B.C. that became an important Roman municipality along the Via Flaminia.

properties.title: Interamnia Praetuttiorum
properties.description: Originally a settlement of the Praetuttii located at the confluence of the rivers Tordino and Vezzola (modern Teramo).

properties.title: Iomnium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 H3 Iomnium

properties.title: Irni
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E4 Irni

properties.title: Isca Dumnoniorum
properties.description: Isca Dumnoniorum (modern Exeter) was a Roman military complex and later town that was the likely base of Legio II Augusta in the first century, prior to its move to Isca Silurum.

properties.title: Isturgi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 A3 Isturgi

properties.title: Isurium
properties.description: Isurium (modern Aldborough) was a Roman settlement - and likely fort - along Dere Street.

properties.title: Italica
properties.description: A city in Spain founded in 206 B.C. by Publius Cornelius Scipio Africanus as a site at which to settle Roman soldiers wounded in the Battle of Ilipa.

properties.title: Iulia Augusta
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 C3 Olbasa/Col. Iulia Augusta

properties.title: Iulia Augusta Felix
properties.alt: Ninica, Col. Iulia Augusta Felix, Claudiopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 C3 Ninica/Col. Iulia Augusta Felix/Claudiopolis

properties.title: Iulia Augusta Hadriana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 E1 Parlais/Col. Iulia Augusta Hadriana

properties.title: Iulia Concordia
properties.description: Iulia Concordia (Concordia Sagittaria) was established by the Romans in 42 BC at the intersection of the Via Annia and the Via Postumia.

properties.title: Iulia Concordia
properties.alt: Brylleion, Myrleia, Apamea, Col. Iulia Concordia
properties.description: Brylleion/Myrleia/Apamea/Col. Iulia Concordia was a city in Bithynia founded originally by the Colophonians.

properties.title: Iulia Constantia Zilil
properties.description: Zilil/Zilis was an Augustan colony of western Mauretania (Iulia Constantia Zilis).

properties.title: Iulia Felix Gemina
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 A1 Lystra/Col. Iulia Felix Gemina

properties.title: Iulia Gemella
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 B4 Acci/Col. Iulia Gemella

properties.title: Iulia Myrtilis
properties.description: An ancient settlement at modern Mértola (Portugal) on the west bank of the Guadiana river (ancient Anas). The town is mentioned in Roman sources, but most of the monumental archaeology dates to the Muslim period or later.

properties.title: Iulia Traducta
properties.description: Iulia Traducta/Iulia Ioza/Tingentera (modern Algeciras, Spain) is an ancient seaport of southern Iberia with Punic and Roman phases.

properties.title: Iuliobriga
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 G2 Iuliobriga

properties.title: Iuliomagus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 E1 Iuliomagus

properties.title: Iuvanum
properties.description: A Roman municipium founded in 87 BC after the Social War, Iuvanum sits on the site of a pre-existing Samnite settlement that occupies an upland plateau.

properties.title: Iuvavum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F2 Iuvavum

properties.title: Kalos Limen
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 23 F3 Kalos Limen?

properties.title: Kellis
properties.description: Roman/late antique settlement, with substantial remains of public religious buildings, including a large temple, residential areas, tombs, and three churches.

properties.title: Krane
properties.description: An ancient settlement near modern Argostoli on the island of Kefalonia.

properties.title: Lamasba
properties.description: Lamasba was a city of Roman Numidia.

properties.title: Lamia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Lamia

properties.title: Lampsacus
properties.description: A Greek city located on the eastern side of the Hellespont.

properties.title: Lancia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 F2 Lancia/Lance

properties.title: Laodicea
properties.description: Laodicea or Laodicea ad Mare (modern Lattakia, Syria) is an ancient city with significant Punic, Greek, and Roman phases.

properties.title: Lapethus
properties.description: Located on the north coast of Cyprus 10 km west of Keryneia, Lapethos/Lepethis Phoinikon is known through archaeological remains for the most part.

properties.title: Laranda
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 C2 Laranda

properties.title: Lares
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 C4 Lares

properties.title: Larinum
properties.description: A settlement of Apulia that originated with the Samnites and Frentani and later came under Roman influence. During the Second Punic War, the Romans defeated Hannibal at Larinum. The settlement eventually gained Roman municipal status.

properties.title: Latopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B3 Latopolis

properties.title: Lavinium
properties.description: An ancient city of Latium on the Numicus river that was founded, according to Virgil, by Aeneas.

properties.title: Lebadea
properties.description: Lebadeia was an ancient settlement located close to the western frontier of Boeotia.

properties.title: Leontini
properties.description: A Greek colonial city founded by Naxians in 729 B.C., Leontini was reduced by Hippocrates of Gela in 498 B.C.

properties.title: Lepti Minus
properties.description: Lepti Minus was an ancient port city in Tunisia.

properties.title: Letopolis
properties.description: Letopolis was an ancient Egyptian city. It served as the capital of the second nome of Lower Egypt.

properties.title: Leucas
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Leucas

properties.title: Libisosa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 C3 Libisosa

properties.title: Lilaea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Lilaia

properties.title: Lilybaeum
properties.description: Lilybaeum (modern Marsala) is located at the extreme westernmost point of Sicily. Lilybaeum was a Punic settlement that was conquered by the Romans in 241 B.C.

properties.title: Limisa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E4 Limisa

properties.title: Limonum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F2 Limonum

properties.title: Limyra
properties.description: An ancient settlement located at modern Yuvalılar in the district of Finike, Antalya province, Turkey.

properties.title: Lindum
properties.description: An Pre-Roman Iron Age settlement of Britain that became the site of a Roman colony after A.D. 71; its full name was Domitiana Lindensium.

properties.title: Lipara
properties.description: A Greek colony and long-time settlement on the island of the same name, located to the north of Sicily in the Tyrrhenian Sea. Modern Lipari.

properties.title: Lissus
properties.description: An important Illyrian port located on the river Drin.

properties.title: Lixus
properties.description: Lixus was an ancient settlement of Mauretania Tingitana.

properties.title: Locri
properties.description: Locri is a town and comune in the province of Reggio Calabria, Calabria, in southern Italy.

properties.title: Lucentum
properties.description: Lucentum was a Phoenician and then Roman settlement in Tarraconensis that was largely abandoned after the third century.

properties.title: Luceria
properties.description: Luceria/Noukeria (Apoulon) was originally a Daunian city. It was named as a Colonia Togata in 320 B.C. and came to play a significant role in the Second Punic War. Constans II sacked the town in A.D. 663.

properties.title: Lucurgentum
properties.description: An ancient city in Hispania Baetica.

properties.title: Lucus Feroniae
properties.description: A sanctuary and grove of Feronia and an associated settlement located along the Via Tiberina in the territory of Capena.

properties.title: Lugdunum Convenarum
properties.description: Lugdunum Convenarum (modern Saint-Bertrand-de-Comminges) was founded by Cnaeus Pompeius as a Roman colony in 72 BC. The site defended the Aran valley and the approaches to Iberia.

properties.title: Luguvalium
properties.description: Luguvalium was a fortified Roman settlement in Britannia.

properties.title: Lupiae
properties.description: A settlement of the indigenous Messapii that was refounded under Roman influence in the third century B.C.

properties.title: Lychnidus
properties.description: Lychnidos (modern Ohrid, Macedonia) is an ancient city listed as UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1979 and 1980 for both its cultural and natural heritage.

properties.title: Lycopolis
properties.description: A city of the west bank of the Nile, Lykopolis/Siout was the capital of the Thirteenth Nome of Upper Egypt (Lycopolites Nome) around 3100 B.C.

properties.title: Macomades
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 37 B1 Euphranta/Macomades

properties.title: Mactaris
properties.description: A Numidian site that eventually became a Roman colony in the second century AD.

properties.title: Madaba
properties.description: Madaba has a long settlement history. During the Roman period the city was part of the Trajanic province of Arabia.

properties.title: Madauros
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 A4 Madauros

properties.title: Magnesia ad Maeandrum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F2 Magnesia ad Maeandrum/Leukophrys

properties.title: Magnesia ad Sipylum
properties.description: Magnesia ad Sipylum was an ancient city in Lydia in Asia Minor (modern Manisa in Turkey). Lucius Cornelius Scipio Asiaticus defeated Antiochus the Great at the battle of Magnesia in 190 BC.

properties.title: Magnum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 D6 Synodion/Magnum

properties.title: Mago
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 inset Mago

properties.title: Malaca
properties.description: A Roman city of Baetica that was perhaps founded by the Carthaginians in the fifth century BC. Modern Malaga, on the Mediterranean coast of Spain.

properties.title: Malvesia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 B6 *Malves(i)a

properties.title: Marruvium
properties.description: Ancient Marruvium was the principal city of the Italic tribe of the Marsi.

properties.title: Mascula Tiberia
properties.description: A major settlement, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 Mascula Tiberia

properties.title: Matilica
properties.description: Matilica was a municipium in Umbria located near the source of the river Aesis and close to the borders of Picenum. The Liber Coloniarum identifies the settlement as one of the “Civitates Piceni.”

properties.title: Mediolanum
properties.description: A town of southwest Gaul founded ca. 20 BC, Mediolanum Santonum was connected with the Roman road network that served Burdigala.

properties.title: Melita
properties.description: A capital town founded by Carthaginians.

properties.title: Mendes
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 F3 Mendes

properties.title: Meninx
properties.description: A harbor settlement (in the vicinity of modern El Kantara) in the southern part of the Tunisian island of Djerba (ancient Meninx/Lotophagitis/Girba).

properties.title: Mesembria
properties.description: Originally a Thracian site known as Menebria, Mesembria was subsequently settled by Dorian Greeks in the early sixth century BC, eventually becoming an important trading colony on the Black Sea coast (modern Nesebar, Bulgaria).

properties.title: Messana
properties.description: Zancle was founded as Greek colony in the eighth century B.C. Home of the Mamertines who called for Roman assistance against Carthaginian forces in 264 B.C., sparking the First Punic War.

properties.title: Metapontum
properties.description: An important city of Magna Graecia, Metapontum was located on the Gulf of Tarentum. The Achaeans were responsible for its foundation, perhaps as early as the eighth century B.C.

properties.title: Methone
properties.description: An ancient settlement sited on the west coast of the Messenian peninsula of the Peloponnese. The collocated modern settlement is called Methoni and is located in the administratively part of the Pylos-Nestoras municipality.

properties.title: Metropolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 E3 Metropolis

properties.title: Metulum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C4 Metulum

properties.title: Mevania
properties.description: Mevania (modern Bevagna) was a Roman municipium in Umbria located on the Via Flaminia. The town was enrolled in the tribus Aemilia.

properties.title: Mevaniola
properties.description: Mevaniola was a Roman city located in the Bidente valley. Its inhabitants were of Umbrian extraction, connected with the settlement of Mevania (Bevagna).

properties.title: Milev
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 E4 Milev

properties.title: Mirobriga
properties.description: Miróbriga (also known as Mirobriga Celticorum) is an ancient Roman town located near the village Santiago do Cacém, Portugal.

properties.title: Mirobriga
properties.description: Mirobriga was a settlement located west of Almadén mentioned by Pliny the Elder.

properties.title: Misenum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F4 Misenum

properties.title: Mogentiana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 E2 Moge(n)tiana

properties.title: Montana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 F6 Montana

properties.title: Mopthensium
properties.description: An ancient settlement and fort known in the 19th and 20th centuries as Henchir el-Ksar. It is located at the eastern foot of the Guellal-ta-Attar where lies the confluence of the Oued Safsaf and the Oued Ghriba.

properties.title: Morgantina
properties.description: An indigenous Sicilian site founded by the Morgeti, according to Strabo, Morgantina was eventually defeated by Syracuse and thus, in turn, passed into Roman hegemony in the third century B.C.

properties.title: Moridunum
properties.description: *Moridunum (Carmarthen) was the civitas capital of the Demetae in Roman Wales.

properties.title: Mothis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 79 inset Mothis

properties.title: Munigua
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E4 Munigua

properties.title: Mursella
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 F4 Mursella

properties.title: Musti
properties.description: Musti was a Roman municipium located along the road from Carthage to Tebessa. The site, known in modern times as Henchir Mest or Henchir Mist, lies just east of the modern town of El Krib in Tunisia's Siliana governorate.

properties.title: Mutina
properties.description: Ancient Mutina (originally the Etruscan town Mutna), was first inhabited by Villanovans and later by Ligurians and the Gaulish Boii. The Boii sieged the city in 218 BC and later Mutina was re-established as a Roman colony (183 BC).

properties.title: Mylasa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 F3 Mylasa

properties.title: Myra
properties.description: An ancient Lycian town

properties.title: Myriandros
properties.description: Myriand(r)os was an ancient Phoenician port near modern İskenderun, Turkey.

properties.title: Mytilene
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 D3 Mytilene

properties.title: Nabrissa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D5 Nabrissa

properties.title: Napoca
properties.description: Napoca (modern Cluj-Napoca) was an ancient Roman settlement established after the conquest of Dacia in A.D. 106. It became a provincial capital of Dacia Porolissensis and was abandoned in the late third century.

properties.title: Narona
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 E6 Narona

properties.title: Naucratis
properties.description: Naucratis was a city of the Nile river delta, located on the Canopic branch. Capital of Ptolemaic Egypt, Naucratis was the site of permanent Greek habitation from the sixth century BC onward.

properties.title: Naupactus
properties.description: Originally a city of the Ozolian Locrians, Naupactus eventually fell to Athens; under Macedonian rule Philip II ceded it to the Aetolians.

properties.title: Nea Pleuron
properties.description: Second site of the ancient Greek city of Pleuron, built after the destruction of the original location in the 3rd century BCE.

properties.title: Neapolis
properties.description: An ancient port city on the coast of Thrace, Neapolis (modern Kavalla in the Decentralized Administration of Macedonia and Thrace in Greece) was probably founded from Thasos in the seventh century CE.

properties.title: Neapolis
properties.description: After the sacking of Jerusalem, Vespasian settled Roman veterans on a new town site located between Mt. Gerizim and Mt. Ebal. The new Roman colony was built on the site of Mabartha.

properties.title: Neapolis
properties.description: Neapolis (modern Nabeul, Tunisia) is a coastal town on the Cap Bon peninsula that was founded by Greeks from Cyrene in the fifth century B.C.

properties.title: Nemausus
properties.description: Nemausus (modern Nîmes, France) was the ancient capital of the Volcae Arecomici and later a Roman colony.

properties.title: Nepet
properties.description: Nepet had become a Roman town before 396 B.C. when Livy dubs it, along with Sutrium, as one of the "keys" to Etruria. It became a Roman colony in 383 B.C. and a municipium after the Social War.

properties.title: Nequinum
properties.description: Nequinum/Narnia (modern Narni) was an ancient settlement in Umbria. It became a Latin colony in 299 BC.

properties.title: Nertobriga Concordia Iulia
properties.description: Nertobriga Concordia Iulia was a Roman municipium.

properties.title: Neviodunum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C4 Neviodunum

properties.title: Nicopolis
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Judea that is mentioned in numerous ancient sources including the Gospel of Luke. Now situated within the modern Canada Park in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, the site was known in modern times as Imwas.

properties.title: Nicopolis ad Istrum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 C5 Nicopolis ad Istrum

properties.title: Nicopolis ad Nestum
properties.description: A settlement founded in Thrace to mark Trajan's victory in the Dacian Wars. It is located on the left bank of the Mesta river.

properties.title: Nora
properties.description: A pre-Roman and Roman site in southern Sardinia.

properties.title: Novae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 C5 Novae

properties.title: Novae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 E6 Novae

properties.title: Novaria
properties.description: A Roman municipium with an orthogonal plan.

properties.title: Noviomagus
properties.description: In Britannia, Noviomagus was the capital of the Regni. According to the Antonine Itinerary, Noviomagus was the first road station along the road from London to Durovernum.

properties.title: Nuceria Alfaterna
properties.description: Nuceria (Alfaterna) (modern Nocera Inferiore) is an ancient settlement in Campania. In pre-Roman times this was the chief settlement of the Sarnus river valley.

properties.title: Nuceria Camellaria
properties.description: Nuceria (modern Nocera Umbra) is an ancient town in Umbria, perhaps founded in the seventh century B.C. by Camerinum.

properties.title: Numantia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 C4 Numantia

properties.title: Numluli
properties.description: A major settlement, cited: BAtlas 32 D3 Numluli

properties.title: Nursia
properties.description: Nursia was the northernmost city of the Sabines.

properties.title: Nysa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 G2 Nysa/Athymbra

properties.title: Oasis Parva
properties.description: The Bahariya Oasis is a depression and oasis located in the Western Desert of Egypt approximately 370 km from Cairo.

properties.title: Obulco
properties.description: Obulco was a Roman municipium of Hispania Baetica.

properties.title: Ocriculum
properties.description: An ancient Umbrian city that became allied with Rome in 308 B.C.

properties.title: Odessus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 E5 Odessus

properties.title: Oeniadae
properties.description: A city at the former mouth of the Achelous river.

properties.title: Oenoanda
properties.description: Oenoanda/Termessos Mikra was an ancient Greek city located in Lycia.

properties.title: Oescus
properties.description: Oescus was originally a Thracian city at the confluence of the Danube and Iskar rivers,

properties.title: Olbia
properties.description: A Phoenician, Carthaginian, and Roman center in northeast Sardinia.

properties.title: Olisipo
properties.description: Olisipo (modern Lisbon, Portugal). Decimus Junius Brutus Callaicus pacified western Iberia and integrated the settlement into the Roman Republic in 138 BC.

properties.title: Omboi
properties.description: The Ptolemaic temple to Sobek and Horus the Elder at Kom Ombo.

properties.title: Oppidum Novum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 C4 Oppidum Novum

properties.title: Orchomenus Arcadicus
properties.description: The so-called "Arcadian Orchomenos", located near a modern village in the Peloponnese that, until the 1960s, was known as Kalpaki.

properties.title: Oropus
properties.description: Oropos was an ancient settlement and port located on the border between Boeotia and Attica, and was source of ongoing disputes in antiquity. Colonists from Eretria founded Oropos.

properties.title: Ortona
properties.description: An ancient site in Italy that most likely originates with the Frentani.

properties.title: Ortoplinia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B5 Ortopla

properties.title: Osca
