properties.description: Osca/Urbs Victrix (modern Huesca) was an Iberian oppidum and capital of the Ilergetes that later became a Roman colony.

properties.title: Osset
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D4 Osset

properties.title: Ossonoba
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 C4 Ossonoba

properties.title: Ostra
properties.description: Ostra was an ancient town of Umbria.

properties.title: Ovilava
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 H4 Ovilava

properties.title: Oxyrhynchus
properties.description: A city of Upper Egypt, Oxyrhynchus/Pemje is particularly well known as an archaeological site that has produced important papyrus manuscripts of ancient texts.

properties.title: Paeania
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 59 C3 Paiania Kathyperthen

properties.title: Paestum
properties.description: A major Greco-Roman center of south Italy, Poseidonia/Paestum was originally founded by Greeks from Sybaris in the seventh century BC, who named the settlement Poseidonia.

properties.title: Palaepaphus
properties.description: Located on Cyprus, Paphos has been inhabited since the Neolithic period and was an important cult center of Aphrodite.

properties.title: Palma
properties.description: The site of Palma, on the island of Majorca, experienced multiple phases of settlement from Talaiotic to Roman.

properties.title: Paraetonium
properties.description: The westernmost town of ancient Egypt.

properties.title: Parentium
properties.description: Ancient Parentium, now Poreč, Croatia, was a city on the western coast of the Istrian peninsula. There is archaeological evidence of Illyrian settlement from the Bronze Age onward. The site factors in Roman campaigns of the second century BC.

properties.title: Parium
properties.description: Founded as a Greek city in the Propontis region of Anatolia, perhaps ca. 709 BCE, Persians would come to control Parium in 546 BCE.

properties.title: Parma
properties.description: The site of a Bronze Age terramare settlement, Parma was re-founded first by Etruscans and later by the Romans when a colony was established there in 183 B.C. The city was rebuilt by Augustus and was eventually sacked by Attila.

properties.title: Patara
properties.description: Patara/Arsinoe was an ancient maritime center of southwest Lycia.

properties.title: Pausulae
properties.description: Pausulae was a town of Picenum mentioned by Pliny the Elder. It was a Municipium of Regio V.

properties.title: Pautalia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 E1 Pautalia

properties.title: Pednelissos
properties.description: Pednelissos was an ancient settlement in Pisidia near to the Eurymedon river.

properties.title: Pellene
properties.description: Pellene was an ancient settlement in Achaea allied with Sparta during the Peloponnesian War.

properties.title: Peltuinum
properties.description: Peltuinum was a settlement of the Vestini located along the Via Claudia Nova. The town is notable as the birthplace of the Roman general Gnaeus Domitius Corbulo.

properties.title: Peparethum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 F2 Peparethos

properties.title: Pessinus
properties.description: An ancient city of Asia Minor, today Ballıhisar in Turkey.

properties.title: Petelia
properties.description: Petelia was an ancient city on the coast of Bruttium in southern Italy. Petelia remained allied to Rome during the Second Punic War.

properties.title: Pharsalus
properties.description: Pharsalus was one of the most important cities of ancient Thessaly.

properties.title: Phaselis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 E4 Phaselis

properties.title: Pheneus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 C2 Pheneos

properties.title: Pheradi Maius
properties.description: Pheradi Maius was a civitas of Africa Proconsularis. The archaeological site is today located near the village of Sidi Khelifa, Tunisia.

properties.title: Pherae
properties.description: Pherai was an ancient city of Thessaly in the district of Pelasgiotis.

properties.title: Phigalia
properties.description: Phigaleia was an ancient city in the southwestern part of Arcadia in Greece.

properties.title: Philadelphia
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Asia Minor, modern Alaşehir, Turkey.

properties.title: Philippeia
properties.description: Pella/Berenice/Philippeia (Tabaqat Fahl (طبقة فحل)) is a ruined ancient city located in northwestern Jordan. Pella was continuously occupied from the Neolithic period until its destruction by means of an earthquake in AD 749.

properties.title: Phintias
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Phintias

properties.title: Phlius
properties.description: An ancient city located in the valley of the river Asopos.

properties.title: Phocaea
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Asia Minor, modern Foça in Turkey.

properties.title: Phoenice
properties.description: Phoinike was an ancient Greek city of Epirus and the location of the Treaty of Phoenice which ended the First Macedonian War.

properties.title: Pinara
properties.description: Pinara was a city of Lycia located at the foot of Mount Cragus (now Mount Babadağ).

properties.title: Pinna
properties.description: Pinna (modern Penne) was a center of the Vestini. The site came under Roman control after the Social War (89 BC).

properties.title: Piraeus
properties.description: The ancient, fortified port settlement (and deme) of ancient Athens.

properties.title: Pisaurum
properties.description: Pisaurum (modern Pésaro) was founded as a Roman colony in the territory of the Picentes in 184 BC.

properties.title: Pitinum Mergens
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 D1 Pitinum Mergens

properties.title: Pitinum Pisaurense
properties.description: An ancient settlement in Umbria, known mostly via its inclusion in the texts of Pliny the Elder and Ptolemy, as well as by means of various epigraphic evidence.

properties.title: Plataea
properties.description: Plataea is an ancient city of Boeotia, located south of Thebes. In 479 BC Plataea was the site of the Battle of Plataea, in which an allied Greek army defeated the Persians.

properties.title: Plestia
properties.description: Plestia was a municipium of the tribus Ufentina.

properties.title: Poetovio
properties.description: Poetovio (Ptuj, Slovenia) was an ancient city in Pannonia that was the base-camp of Legio XIII Gemina.

properties.title: Pola
properties.description: A port city on the Adriatic Sea (modern Pula in Croatia).

properties.title: Pollentia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 inset Pollentia

properties.title: Polyrrhenium
properties.description: Polyrrenia was an important ancient city of western Crete.

properties.title: Pompeii
properties.description: An ancient city of Campania destroyed by the volcanic eruption of Mt. Vesuvius in A.D. 79, Pompeii was named as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1997.

properties.title: Pompeiopolis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 66 F3 Soloi/Pompeiopolis

properties.title: Pontia
properties.description: A settlement on Pontia Ins. The largest of the Pontine Islands, Pontia lies 33 km south of Cape Circeo on the Italian mainland.

properties.title: Populonium
properties.description: Populonia likely derives its name from the Etruscan god 'Fufluns' and was an important maritime and commercial center of Etruria located in the Colline Metallifere.

properties.title: Porolissum
properties.description: Porolissum was a Roman city in Dacia established in A.D. 106. It became the capital of Dacia Porolissensis in A.D. 124.

properties.title: Potentia
properties.description: A city settled in an area subjugated by Rome during the fourth century B.C. The local populace rebelled against Rome after the defeat at Cannae (216 B.C.), however the defeat of the Carthaginians eventually led to Potentia's reincorporation.

properties.title: Potentia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C3 Potentia

properties.title: Privernum
properties.description: A Volscian center that Rome reportedly destroyed in the late fourth century B.C.

properties.title: Prusias ad Hypium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 B3 Prusias ad Hypium/Kieros

properties.title: Ptolemais Hermiou
properties.description: A city founded by Ptolemy I Soter as the capital of Upper Egypt.

properties.title: Pupput
properties.description: Pupput was an ancient city of Africa proconsularis.

properties.title: Pyrgi
properties.description: Pyrgi is an ancient Etruscan port city on the Tyrrhenian coast of Italy. A Roman colony was established at the site by 191 B.C. In 1964 the bi-lingual 'Pyrgi Plaques' were discovered during the course of excavations.

properties.title: Quiza Cenitana
properties.description: Quiza Cenitana was a place located on the coast of Mauretania Caesariensis. Various ancient authors refer to it by different terms - colonia (Ptolemy), municipium (Antonine Itinerary), and oppidum (Pliny the Elder).

properties.title: Raphia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 E3 Raphia

properties.title: Rapidum
properties.description: Rapidum was a Roman camp of the Mauretanian limes.

properties.title: Ratae Corieltauvorum
properties.description: Ratae Corieltauvorum (modern Leicester) was a settlement in Britannia.

properties.title: Ratiaria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 E6 Ratiaria

properties.title: Ravenna
properties.description: A city of northern Adriatic Italy that served as the capital of the Western Roman Empire from A.D. 402 until 476.

properties.title: Regina
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E3 Regina

properties.title: Regium
properties.description: A site with settlement origins in the Bronze and Iron ages, Rhegion was eventually colonized by Chalcis in either 730 or 743 BC.

properties.title: Remesiana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 21 E6 Remesiana

properties.title: Rhamnus
properties.description: Rhamnous was an ancient settlement of Attica adjacent to the Euboean Strait.

properties.title: Rhithymna
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 B2 Rhithymna/Arsinoe

properties.title: Rhodus
properties.description: A settlement on the island of Rhodes.

properties.title: Rider
properties.description: The chief town of the Dalmatae.

properties.title: Ris(...)
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 G6 Ris(...)

properties.title: Risinium
properties.description: A city on the Adriatic coast of Dalmatia with a protected harbor.

properties.title: Rusaddir
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 29 C1 Rusaddir

properties.title: Rusazus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 A3 Rusazus?

properties.title: Ruscino
properties.description: Col. Ruscino was an Iberian oppidum and town of Narbonese Gaul.

properties.title: Rusellae
properties.description: An Etruscan city captured by Rome in 294 B.C., Rusellae's >2 mile circuit of megalithic walls represent an important early instance of stone fortification walling in the Italian peninsula.

properties.title: Rusguniae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 F3 Rusguniae

properties.title: Rusicada
properties.description: Rusicade/Thapsus was the port of Cirta and a Roman colonia, located at the mouth of the small river Thapsus.

properties.title: Rusippisir
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 H3 Rusippisir

properties.title: Rusuccuru
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 G3 Rusuccuru

properties.title: Saccaea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 E4 Sakkaia/Maximianopolis

properties.title: Sacili
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 A4 Sacili

properties.title: Saena
properties.description: Saena was an Etruscan city that received a Roman colony (Saena Iulia) in the time of Augustus.

properties.title: Saepinum
properties.description: An ancient town of Samnium captured by the Romans in 293 B.C., located on the road between Beneventum and Corfinium.

properties.title: Saetabis
properties.description: Saetabis was a town of the Contestani in Hispania Tarraconensis. It has been identified with the modern Spanish town of Xàtiva in the province of Valencia.

properties.title: Saettae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 A4 Saittai

properties.title: Saguntum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 E2 Saguntum/Arse

properties.title: Sais
properties.description: Sais was an ancient Egyptian town located in the Western Nile Delta on the Canopic branch of the Nile river. It served as the provincial capital of Sap-Meh, the fifth nome of Lower Egypt, and became a capital during the Twenty-fourth Dynasty.

properties.title: Sala
properties.description: In and around the area where the Islamic-era cemetery of Chellah now lies in modern Rabat, Morocco, a Phoenician and Carthaginian settlement developed. The Roman colony of Sala was eventually established there.

properties.title: Salacia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 B3 Salacia (Imperatoria)

properties.title: Salaria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 B4 Salaria?

properties.title: Saldae
properties.description: Founded by Numidian Berbers, the settlement of Saldae (modern Béjaïa, Algeria) was a minor port during phases of Carthaginian and Roman occupation. The Roman emperor Vespasian established a veteran colony there.

properties.title: Salernum
properties.description: A city of Campania with Osco-Etruscan origins that became a Roman colony in 197 B.C.

properties.title: Salmantica
properties.description: Salmantica (modern Salamanca) was an indigenous Celtic center before falling to the Carthaginians and, later, the Romans.

properties.title: Salpensa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E4 Sal(pens)a

properties.title: Salvium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 D5 Salvium

properties.title: Samos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 D2 Samos

properties.title: Samothrace
properties.description: The homonymous settlement on the Aegean island, located on its north shore (modern Paleopolis).

properties.title: Sarsina
properties.description: Sarsina was a town of the Umbri that was captured by Cornelius Scipio in 271 B.C. and was the birthplace of the Latin author Plautus. The necropolis at Sarsina has been extensively studied.

properties.title: Saticula
properties.description: Saticula was a Samnite site in Campania that received a Latin colony in 313 B.C.

properties.title: Saturnia
properties.description: An Etruscan town that Dionysius of Halicarnassus claimed had been founded by the Pelasgians, Saturnia received a Roman colony in 183 BC.

properties.title: Savaria
properties.description: Colonia Claudia Savaria (Szombathely) Hungary.

properties.title: Saxetanum
properties.description: Sexi/Saxetanum (modern Almuñécar) was a Phoenician colony established ca. 800 BC that eventually became a Roman one (Sexi Firmum Iulium).

properties.title: Scarbantia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 D2 Scarbantia

properties.title: Scardona
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 C6 Scardona

properties.title: Scodra
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 B1 Scodra

properties.title: Sebaste
properties.description: Samaria/Sebaste was an ancient city of Iudaea. In 30 B.C. it passed under the control of Herod the Great and was renamed Sebaste in honor of the emperor Augustus.

properties.title: Sebaste
properties.description: Elaioussa/Sebaste was an ancient Roman city in Asia Minor founded in the second century BC.

properties.title: Sebaste
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 64 E1 Sebasteia/Megalopolis?/Talaura?

properties.title: Sebennytus
properties.description: Sebennytos was an ancient city of Lower Egypt located on the Sebennytic branch of the Nile river. It was the capital of Lower Egypt's twelfth nome (the Sebennyte nome).

properties.title: Segeda
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D4 Segeda

properties.title: Segermes
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 F4 Segermes

properties.title: Segesta
properties.description: A center of the Elymian people in northwestern Sicily.

properties.title: Segida
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D3 Segida

properties.title: Segodunum
properties.description: Segodunum was a Gallic oppidum and later a Gallo-Roman town.

properties.title: Segovia
properties.description: Segovia, originally of Celtiberian origin, became a Roman settlement. It is perhaps the site where Metellus defeated Hirtuleius, the general of Sertorius, in 75 B.C.

properties.title: Seleucia ad Calycadnum
properties.description: A city founded by Seleucus Nicator between 296 and 280 BC.

properties.title: Seleucia ad Pyramum
properties.description: Mopsou(h)estia/Seleukeia pros to Pyramo was an ancient settlement in Cilicia Pedias (now modern Yakapınar, Turkey).

properties.title: Selge
properties.description: Selge was an important city of Pisidia; Strabo claims it was founded as a Spartan colony.

properties.title: Sena Gallica
properties.description: Located at the mouth of the river Misa, Sena Gallica was founded by the Senonian Gauls in the fourth century B.C. and became the site of Rome's earliest Adriatic colony before 280 B.C. Pompeius Magnus destroyed the city in 82 B.C.

properties.title: Senia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B5 Senia

properties.title: Sentinum
properties.description: An ancient town in the region of Italy now known as Le Marche, Sentinum is perhaps best known for the pivotal Battle of Sentinum of the Third Samnite War that took place nearby in 295 BC.

properties.title: Septempeda
properties.description: Septempeda was an ancient settlement of Picenum. Its Roman phase began as a conciliabulum in the third century B.C.

properties.title: Seressi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E4 *Seressi

properties.title: Seria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D3 Seria

properties.title: Sestinum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 C1 Sestinum

properties.title: Sestos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 51 G4 Sestos

properties.title: Setia
properties.description: A Volscian settlement of the fifth century B.C. that became a Latin colony in 382 B.C.

properties.title: Siarum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 E4 Siarum

properties.title: Sicca Veneria
properties.description: Sicca Veneria was an important Punic and Roman city located in what is now Tunisia.

properties.title: Sidyma
properties.description: A town of Lycia located on the southern slope of Mt. Cragus and northwest of the mouth of the Xanthus river.

properties.title: Siga
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 29 D1 Siga

properties.title: Signia
properties.description: A city of Latium Adjectum established as a Latin colony ca. 494 B.C.

properties.title: Silandos
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 A4 Silandos

properties.title: Sillyum
properties.description: Sillyon was an ancient Graeco-Pamphylian fortress and city near Attaleia in Pamphylia, on the southern coast Asia Minor.

properties.title: Simitthus
properties.description: A city of Africa Proconsularis, Simitthu is especially noted for its marble quarries.

properties.title: Singidunum
properties.description: An ancient settlement and Roman fort on the site of modern Belgrade in Serbia.

properties.title: Sinuessa
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E3 Sinues(s)a/Sinope

properties.title: Sipontum
properties.description: A Greek colony that fell first under Samnite control, then was later taken by Alexander of Epirus. The site became a Roman colony in 189 BC.

properties.title: Soli
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B2 Soloi/Aipeia?

properties.title: Soluntum
properties.description: An ancient settlement on the north-west coast of Sicily, some 16 km east of modern Palermo.

properties.title: Sora
properties.description: An ancient city of Latium located in the valley of the Liris river that likely had Volscian origins. The Romans captured the city in 345 BC and later established a colony there in 303 BC after the Second Samnite War.

properties.title: Splonum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 D5 Splonum

properties.title: Stobi
properties.description: An ancient town of Paeonia that eventually became the capital of the Roman province of Macedonia Salutaris.

properties.title: Stratus
properties.description: Stratos was the chief town of Acarnania. Modern Sourovigli in Greece.

properties.title: Stymphalum
properties.description: Stymphalos was a small but wealthy city of ancient Arcadia.

properties.title: Suasa
properties.description: Suasa was the site of a Roman municipium in Umbria. It lies in the Piano Volpello along a road from Cales to Sena Gallica. Alaric razed the settlement in 409.

properties.title: Suel
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 A5 Suel

properties.title: Suessa Aurunca
properties.description: The ancient center of the Aurunci, Suessa Aurunca was abandoned in 337 B.C. for a more favorable site (modern Sessa) where a Latin colony was established in 313 B.C.

properties.title: Suessula
properties.description: Suessula was an important ancient city of Campania.

properties.title: Sufasar
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 E4 Sufasar

properties.title: Sufes
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 D1 Sufes

properties.title: Sufetula
properties.description: Sufetula, the modern Sbeitla, was founded in the later first century A.D. and was located at a major road junction.

properties.title: Sulcis
properties.description: A site with Phoenician origins (founded ca. 730-700 B.C.) located in southwestern Sardinia. Site of a Roman naval victory against Carthage by Gaius Sulpicius Paterculus in 258 BC during the First Punic War.

properties.title: Sulmo
properties.description: A chief settlement of the Peligni that was ravaged by the Carthaginians during the Second Punic War.

properties.title: Superaequum
properties.description: One of three towns of the Paeligni which possessed municipal rights.

properties.title: Sutrium
properties.description: An ancient settlement along the line of what would become the Roman Via Cassia and described by Livy, together with Nepet, as one of the keys to Etruria. A Latin colony was founded at the site after the fall of Veii.

properties.title: Syedra
properties.description: A site in Cilicia Aspera near Demirtaş.

properties.title: Syene
properties.description: The ancient city of Swenett in Egypt, called Syene by the Greeks.

properties.title: Synnada
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 E4 Synnada

properties.title: Tadinae
properties.description: Tadinae (Sant'Antonio della Rasina to the west of Gualdo Tadino) is an ancient municipium of Umbria and was a road station on the Via Flaminia.

properties.title: Tanagra
properties.description: Tanagra was an ancient settlement of Boeotia.

properties.title: Tanis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 F2 Tanis

properties.title: Tarsatica
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 20 B4 Tarsatica

properties.title: Taucheira
properties.description: Arsinoe/Taucheira (modern Tocra) may have originally been a Greek foundation that was part of Pentapolis of Cyrenaica. It eventually became a Roman colony and, by the time of Justinian, was fortified.

properties.title: Tauromenium
properties.description: Tauromenium was an ancient city in Sicily founded in 358 B.C.

properties.title: Tavium
properties.description: The main settlement of the Galatian tribe of the Trocmi, it was inhabited from the fourth millennium BCE.

properties.title: Teanum Apulum
properties.description: A town in ancient Apulia located between Larinum and and Sipontum. Rome took control of the site in 318 BC, granting it municipal status.

properties.title: Teate Marrucinorum
properties.description: Originally named Teate Marrucinorum, Teate (modern Chieti) was the principal settlement of the Marrucini. Strabo attributes its origins to Arcadian Greeks.

properties.title: Telesia
properties.description: An ancient Samnite city located in the valley of the Calor river.

properties.title: Telmessus
properties.description: Telmessos (modern Fethiye) was an ancient city of Lycia.

properties.title: Tentyris
properties.description: The Ptolemaic temple to Hathor at Dendera.

properties.title: Tergestum
properties.description: Tergeste (the modern Trieste) was first a vicus dependent on Aquileia (ca. 100 BC) and later became a colony under Caesar, sometime before 52 B.C.

properties.title: Termessus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D4 Termessos

properties.title: Terventum
properties.description: Terventum was the site of a Roman municipium established in the territory of the Pentrian Samnites.

properties.title: Teurnia
properties.description: Teurnia, on the Drau river, was an ancient settlement of Noricum.

properties.title: Teus
properties.description: An ancient Ionian settlement of Asia Minor, located at modern Siğacik in Turkey.

properties.title: Thabraca
properties.description: Thabraca was a maritime city of Numidia located at the mouth of the river Tusca.

properties.title: Thaenae
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 G3 Thaenae

properties.title: Thagaste
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 A4 Thagaste

properties.title: Thamugadi
properties.description: A Roman colonial town founded in the Aurès Mountains of Algeria by the Emperor Trajan around AD 100. The city's full name was Colonia Marciana Ulpia Traiana Thamugadi. Trajan named the city in commemoration of his mother Marcia.

properties.title: Thapsus
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 H1 Thapsus

properties.title: Tharrus
properties.description: An ancient Phoenician settlement located in western Sardinia on a promontory in the Gulf of Oristano, just south of modern San Giovanni di Sinis.

properties.title: Thasos
properties.description: Greeks from Paros founded a colony on Thasos in the middle of the seventh century B.C.

properties.title: Thaumacia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C2 Thaumakoi

properties.title: Thebae Phthiotides
properties.description: A city located nend of the ancient Krokian plain

properties.title: Thebes
properties.description: Diospolis Magna/Thebai was known in ancient Egypt as Waset, "City of the Sceptre". The site was designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1979.

properties.title: Thelepte
properties.description: Thelepte was a Roman city in Africa Byzacena. It achieved municipal status perhaps in the time of Vespasian and became a colonia under Trajan. The city played an important administrative role in fourth century AD north Africa.

properties.title: Thespiae
properties.description: Thespiai was an ancient city of Boeotia, located at the foot of Mt. Helicon.

properties.title: Theveste
properties.description: Theveste (modern Tébessa, Algeria) became a Roman center and, in the Imperial period, the Legio III Augusta was raised there. In later times Theveste is connected with the Donatists.

properties.title: Thignica
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D3 Thignica

properties.title: Thisbe
properties.description: Thisbe was an ancient town of Boeotia located on the southern flank of Mt. Helicon.

properties.title: Thisica
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 E3 Thisiduo

properties.title: Thmuis
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 74 F3 Thmouis

properties.title: Thuburbo Maius
properties.description: An ancient settlement with extensive archaeological remains, located north across the river Kebir from the modern city of El Fahs in Tunisia.

properties.title: Thuburnica
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 B3 Thuburnica

properties.title: Thubursicum Bure
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 32 D4 Thubursicu Bure

properties.title: Thubursicum Numidarum
properties.description: Thubursicum Numidarum (now Khamissa, Algeria) is an indigenous site that became a Roman municipium by A.D. 100 (Municipium Ulpium Traianum Augustum Thubursicu).

properties.title: Thugga
properties.description: Thugga/Tokai? (Dougga) is an ancient site in Tunisia with a rich heritage that includes Numidian-Berber, Punic, ancient Roman, and Byzantine phases.

properties.title: Thurii
properties.description: Sybaris was an ancient city of Magna Graecia founded as an Achaean colony as early as 720 B.C. on a plain drained by both the Crati and Sybaris rivers.

properties.title: Thyatira
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Asia Minor, located at modern Akhisar in Turkey. Visible remains today are limited; they include the archaeological area of Tepe Mezarı in the center of the modern town.

properties.title: Thyrium
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Thyrreion

properties.title: Thysdrus
properties.description: Thysdrus (modern El Djem, Tunisia) was a Punic and Roman city of North Africa. It flourished in the second century A.D. and built its massive amphitheater in the third century.

properties.title: Tiberias
properties.description: Tiberias was founded by Herod Antipas ca. 20 BC.

properties.title: Tibiscum
properties.description: Tibiscum was an ancient settlement of Dacia mentioned by Ptolemy that later became a Roman castrum and municipium.

properties.title: Tifernum Tiberinum
properties.description: An Umbrian hilltown in the floodplain of the Tiber river north of Perusia.

properties.title: Tigava
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 30 C4 Tigava Municipium

properties.title: Tipasa
properties.description: Tipasa was an ancient Punic trading-post conquered by Rome and turned into a strategic base for the conquest of the kingdoms of Mauritania.

properties.title: Tithorea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D3 Tithorea/Neon

properties.title: Tius
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 C2 Tios/Tieion

properties.title: Tlos
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Lykia, located two kilometers due east of the modern village of Düğer in Turkey's Mugla province.

properties.title: Tolentinum
properties.description: Tolentinum was a town of Picenum that is listed as having municipal status in the Liber Coloniarum.

properties.title: Toletum
properties.description: Ancient Toletum, located on the Tagus river, was the main settlement of the Carpetani. The Romans captured the city in 193 B.C. In 507, Toletum became the capital of the Visigothic Kingdom and in 711 it fell to Muslim invaders.

properties.title: Tolosa
properties.description: Tolosa (modern Toulouse) was a city of Gallia Narbonensis and the metropolis of the Volcae Tectosages.

properties.title: Torone
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 51 B5 Torone

properties.title: Tralles
properties.alt: Caesarea, Dia, Seleuceia ad Maeandrum, Kaisareia
properties.description: Tralles/Dia/Seleuceia ad Maeandrum/Kaisareia was an ancient city founded by Argives and Trallians that then fell under the sway of the Persian Empire and, later, Hellenistic kings.

properties.title: Trapezus
properties.description: An ancient settlement of Asia Minor on the south coast of the Black Sea. Modern Trabzon in Turkey.

properties.title: Trea
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 E2 Trea

properties.title: Treba
properties.description: Treba (modern Trevi nel Lazio) was originally a settlement of the Aequi.

properties.title: Trebenna
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D4 Trebenna

properties.title: Trebula Balliensis
properties.description: An ancient city of Campania that became enfranchised in the Roman system in the late fourth century B.C.

properties.title: Trebula Mutuesca
properties.description: Trebula Mutuesca or Trebula Mutusca was one of two Sabine cities bearing the name Trebula.

properties.title: Tridentum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 I1 Trident(i)um

properties.title: Tripolis ad Maeandrum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 A1 Tripolis ad Maeandrum/Apollonia ad Maeandrum/Antoniopolis

properties.title: Troesmis
properties.description: Troesmis was an ancient town of Scythia Minor and was connected, at various times, with Legio V Macedonica and Legio II Herculia.

properties.title: Troezen
properties.description: An ancient settlement, located in the Peloponnese.

properties.title: Tropaeum Traiani
properties.description: Civitas Tropaensium was a Roman castrum settled by Roman veterans of the Dacian Wars. Constantine I rebuilt the city after it was destroyed by the Goths and it survived until the Avars sacked it in A.D. 587.

properties.title: Tubusuctu
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 31 B3 Tubusuctu

properties.title: Tucci
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 B4 Tucci/Col. Augusta Gemella

properties.title: Tuder
properties.description: Tuder, modern Todi, is an ancient city of Umbria. Its foundation likely occurred during the eighth/seventh centuries BC, although legendary traditions associate the site with the activities of Herakles.

properties.title: Tuficum
properties.description: Tuficum was an ancient city of Umbria located on the Aesis river. It became a municipium of the tribus Oufentina.

properties.title: Turris Libisonis
properties.description: A settlement of northwestern Sardinia, located at the mouth of the Mannu River. The site served as a port in Phoenician, Carthaginian, and Roman phases of occupation. The modern Porto Torres stands on the site, about 10 miles distant from Sassari.

properties.title: Turris Tamalleni
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 A1 Turris Tamalleni

properties.title: Tuscana
properties.description: An ancient city that traced its legendary origins to Ascanius, son of Aeneas. The site became an important Etruscan mercantile center and eventually achieved Roman municipal status in 88 B.C.

properties.title: Tusculum
properties.description: Tusculum was a Latin city of the Alban Hills in central Italy. After 381 BC Tusculum became the first "municipium cum suffragio".

properties.title: Tyana
properties.description: An ancient city of Cappadocia that was the capital of the Neo-Hittite kingdom in the first millennium B.C.

properties.title: Tyndaris
properties.description: An ancient city on the north coast of Sicily. Modern Tindari.

properties.title: Uchi Maius
properties.description: Uchi Maius was a township of Africa Proconsularis where Marius settled veterans. It did not achieve the status of colonia until the third century.

properties.title: Ucubi
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 A4 Ucubi/Col. Claritas Iulia

properties.title: Ugia
properties.description: U(r)gia was located 27 Roman miles northwest of Hasta Regia.

properties.title: Ulia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 F4 Ulia (Fidentia)

properties.title: Ulpia Noviomagus
properties.description: An ancient settlement of the Batavi. Modern Nijmegen in the Netherlands.

properties.title: Ulpia Traiana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G1 Col. Ulpia Traiana/Tricensimae

properties.title: Ulpiana
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 49 D1 Ulpiana

properties.title: Uluzibbira
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 33 F1 Uluzibbira

properties.title: Urbs Salvia
properties.description: An ancient city of Regio V (Picenum) where a Roman colony was founded in the second century B.C.

properties.title: Urgao
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 A4 Urgao/Vircao

properties.title: Urvinum Hortense
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 D3 Urvinum Hortense

properties.title: Urvinum Mataurense
properties.description: Urvinum Mataurense (modern Urbino).

properties.title: Uthina
properties.description: Uthina was a town in the province of Africa Proconsularis with Roman colonial status.

properties.title: Utica
properties.description: A Punic center located west of Carthage.

properties.title: Vaga
properties.description: Vaga (Béja, Tunisia) was a much-contested city in antiquity. During the conflict against Rome, Iugurtha made Vaga his base of operations.

properties.title: Valentia
properties.description: A settlement of the Segovellauni in Gallia Narbonensis.

properties.title: Valentia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 E2 Valentia

properties.title: Valeria
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 27 C2 Valeria

properties.title: Veii
properties.description: An important city of South Etruria located north-northwest of Rome, Veii was eventually sacked by the Romans in 396 B.C. The ancient city covered some 180 ha.

properties.title: Velia
properties.description: The ancient city of Elea / Velia was said to have been founded by Phocaean Greeks in the sixth century B.C. and then became an ally of Rome in the third century B.C.

properties.title: Velitrae
properties.description: Velitrae (modern Velletri) was an ancient Volscian settlement. In the Roman Republican period, Velitrae was attacked by Marcus Furius Camillus (379 B.C.) and again by Cincinnatus (377 B.C.); the Romans controlled the site by 365 B.C.

properties.title: Venafrum
properties.description: Venafrum was an ancient town of Campania.

properties.title: Venta Belgarum
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 F3 Venta

properties.title: Venta Icenorum
properties.description: Venta or Venta Icenorum was a central place, and perhaps market-town, of the Iceni, the tribe that revolted against Roman rule in 61 CE.

properties.title: Venusia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C3 Venusia

properties.title: Vercellae
properties.description: Originally a captial of the Libici, a tribe of Ligurians, Vercellae became an important Roman municipium.

properties.title: Verulae
properties.description: Verulae is an ancient city that became a Roman municipium in 90 B.C. Modern Veroli in Italy.

properties.title: Verulamium
properties.description: A Roman settlement located southwest of modern St Albans, Hertfordshire, England.

properties.title: Vesontio
properties.description: Vesontion (modern Besançon) is first mentioned by Caesar in his commentaries ca. 58 BC. It is situated on a tributary of the Saône river.

properties.title: Vesunna
properties.description: Vesunna (Périgueux) was an oppidum of the Petrucorii that became part of Roman Aquitania.

properties.title: Vettona
properties.description: Vettona (modern Bettona) is an ancient settlement of Umbria located between Perusia and Mevania. Pliny the Elder lists it among the municipalities of Umbria, and it also numbered as one of the “xv Populi Umbriae".

properties.title: Vetulonia
properties.description: The Etruscan Vatluna resisted Rome's influence in the seventh century, although the city may have been the source of Rome's adoption of the curule chair and the fasces.

properties.title: Vibo Valentia
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 46 D4 Hipponion/Vibo Valentia

properties.title: Vicetia
properties.description: Vicetia (modern Vicenza) as a settlement of the Veneti in northern Italy.

properties.title: Vindobona
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 13 B4 Mun. Vindobona

properties.title: Viroconium
properties.description: Viroconium was a large and prosperous city of Roman Britain.

properties.title: Virtus Iulia
properties.description: An archaeological site on the border between the modern Spanish municipalities of Castro del Río and Baena with evidence for late Roman-Republican, early Roman-Imperial, and medieval and post-medieval occupation.

properties.title: Visentium
properties.description: Visentium (Bisenzio) was an Etruscan settlement located on the shores of Lago di Bolsena. The site has yielded especially important evidence for Iron Age and Etruscan period burials.

properties.title: Volaterrae
properties.description: A major Etruscan city of North Etruria.

properties.title: Volturnum
properties.description: Volturnum was located at the mouth of the Volturno river.

properties.title: Volubilis
properties.description: A town of Mauretania Tingitana and a Roman colony.

properties.title: Xanthus
properties.description: An ancient city of Lycia.

properties.title: Zama Regia
properties.description: Zama Regia/Zama *Maior in Africa Proconsularis was the site of the Battle of Zama on October 19, 202 B.C., at the close of the Second Punic War fought between Rome and Carthage.

properties.title: Zarai
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 D2 Zarai

properties.title: Zeugma
properties.description: An ancient settlement whose site is located in the Gaziantep province of modern Turkey, at the modern village of Belkis (a.k.a. Kavunlu). The majority of the ancient remains were covered by water in the year 2000.

properties.title: Zitha
properties.description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 D1 Zitha

properties.title: Zucchabar
properties.description: Zucchabar was an ancient city of Mauretania Caesariensis with Punic origins. The modern Algerian community of Miliana lies atop and around the largely unexcavated ancient site.
